title: Margaret Atwood says the problem with AI is ‘garbage in, garbage out’
slides: Margaret Atwood warns AI fails silently—when does garbage become dangerous?
Her concern: AI trained on flawed human data amplifies errors.
She argues language models cannot judge truth, only patterns.
You now must audit outputs yourself, not trust automation.
Test one AI claim today against a primary source.
thread_text:
1. AI fails silently

Margaret Atwood warns AI can appear correct while it quietly amplifies toxic errors from flawed human data.

2. Trained on broken data

Language models can't judge truth—they only detect patterns in the garbage humans feed them.

3. Pattern, not truth

When an AI sounds confident, it might just be repeating biased correlations, not facts.

4. Your audit, not theirs

You must check outputs yourself—automation won't catch its own mistakes.

5. Test one claim today

Take an AI statement and verify it against a primary source. Did the truth hold?

instagram.caption:
Margaret Atwood says AI fails silently: it can sound confident while running on flawed human data.

She warns these models can't judge truth—only detect patterns in the garbage we feed them. That means errors get amplified without warning. ⚠️

Your job just changed: you must audit outputs yourself, automation won't catch its own mistakes. 🔍

Pick one AI claim you used today. Did you verify it against a primary source—or just trust the pattern?

carousel_slides.description: AI silently amplifies toxic errors from flawed human data, warns Margaret Atwood.
Language models only detect patterns in broken human data—they cannot judge truth.
Atwood warns a confident AI may just repeat biased correlations, not facts.
You must check outputs yourself—automation won't catch its own mistakes.
Test one AI claim today against a primary source. Did the truth hold?
